channel.title: Ziyad Mir — Writing
channel.description: Essays on content understanding, AI moderation and governance, deployment control, agent runtimes, and engineering judgment.

item.title: After Intelligence Gets Cheap, Deployment Control Becomes the Bottleneck
item.description: A technical thesis on the control plane between model capability and production action: policy evidence, eval gates, permission matrices, agent runtimes, moderation workflows, and operating metrics.

item.title: AI-for-Work: A Systems Guide to Governed AI Deployment
item.description: A systems view of enterprise AI deployment: IDEs, MCP gateways, observability, orchestration, knowledge preservation, and work-as-code.

item.title: Proof of Concept: Coding an MCP Client with Claude
item.description: Claude Code built an MCP client for NBA stats with human steering, exposing both the leverage and the brittleness of agentic coding.

item.title: Building an Autonomous MCP Server with HITL Guidance
item.description: An AI assistant can build a production-style MCP server quickly, but human-in-the-loop guidance still determines whether the result is useful.

item.title: When "Move Fast" Meets "Move Safely" - Reconciling the Startup Builder Mindset with Enterprise Reality
item.description: Enterprise AI work needs startup speed translated into compliance-aware execution instead of naive wall-breaking.

item.title: The Bounty Economy: When AI Gets Stuck, Humans Get Paid
item.description: Future work may become bounty-based human escalation for AI agents that need judgment, taste, or local context.

item.title: The Future of Work: AI Guidance and the New Engineering Paradigm
item.description: Engineering work may shift toward guiding cloud agents from lightweight interfaces rather than doing every implementation step manually.

item.title: The Future of Engineering Resource Allocation: From Headcount to Compute Credits
item.description: Agentic engineering could make compute credits more important than headcount allocation inside technical organizations.

item.title: Software Creatures: Imagining the Next Evolution of Systems
item.description: Future systems may look like temporary agentic creatures rather than permanent services: assembled for a goal, instrumented, then dissolved or evolved.

item.title: Part 1: Get Everyone an AI IDE - The Foundation of Enterprise AI
item.description: The first enterprise AI move should be broad AI IDE deployment, with security and adoption handled as first-class work.

item.title: Part 2: Building a Centralized MCP Gateway - The Context Layer
item.description: A centralized MCP gateway gives enterprise AI tools safe access to organizational context without turning every integration into a one-off exception.

item.title: Part 3: From Proxy Logs to Intelligence - Enterprise AI Observability
item.description: Proxy-level AI logs can become the foundation for adoption metrics, productivity insights, cost control, and security monitoring.

item.title: Part 4: Agent Orchestration at Scale - Building an Enterprise Platform
item.description: Enterprise agents need orchestration, reliability, governance, and platform-level controls before they can run meaningful workflows at scale.

item.title: Part 5: Preserving Institutional Knowledge in the Age of AI
item.description: Retention policies erase useful context, so organizations need selective preservation systems that keep the reasoning behind important work.

item.title: Part 6: Work-as-Code: The Micro-MCP Revolution
item.description: Engineering tasks should become small programmable interfaces that AI agents can invoke instead of informal human-only rituals.

item.title: Part 7: Building AI Observability and Adoption Programs
item.description: AI adoption needs dashboards, code labs, power-user discovery, and feedback loops that turn scattered usage into organizational learning.

item.title: AI and Layoffs: The Future of Work in the Age of Automation
item.description: A landing page for the AI-and-layoffs series.

item.title: AI, Layoffs, and the Capability Gradient
item.description: AI changes who gets displaced first by shifting value toward people who can operate at higher levels of ambiguity and leverage.

item.title: The Evolution of Tech Leadership: How Senior ICs and Managers Are Reshaping Organizations
item.description: Senior ICs and managers are converging as AI and complexity reshape what technical leadership means.

item.title: Knowing vs Figuring Out: Why Humans Still Matter in the Age of AI
item.description: Humans remain valuable because they define and frame problems while AI answers well-posed prompts.

item.title: Engineering Across Cultures: Lessons from Global Teams
item.description: Global engineering teams need explicit handling of cultural communication, decision-making, and operating differences.

item.title: Scaling Engineering Teams: Lessons from Tech Giants
item.description: Scaling engineering orgs requires structure, autonomy, communication systems, and process discipline without crushing local ownership.

item.title: Designing Systems for Ultra-High Scale
item.description: High-scale systems require multi-dimensional architecture across throughput, data, geography, operations, and organizational ownership.

item.title: Integrating Machine Learning in Large-Scale Products
item.description: Production ML succeeds through architecture, data pipelines, monitoring, and integration discipline rather than model quality alone.

item.title: Building Brand Identity Through Digital Advertising
item.description: Brand advertising requires identity systems, measurement, and technical campaign infrastructure rather than only direct-response optimization.

item.title: Part 1: Going One Layer Deeper - Why Granular Metrics Matter
item.description: Granular metrics reveal optimization opportunities that aggregate metrics usually hide.

item.title: The Knowledge Boundary Illusion: Why Deltas Get Harder to See
item.description: As models approach or exceed your knowledge, capability gaps become harder to articulate because the boundary moves beyond your own inspection ability.

item.title: The Coming Two-Tiered Internet
item.description: AI content may split the internet into free slop-heavy feeds and paid or trusted spaces where human curation matters more.

item.title: From Cat Videos to Cutting-Edge Signals: The Hidden Value in Social Platforms
item.description: Noisy social platforms can contain rare, high-value, real-time signals if you know how to filter them.

item.title: Generative Worlds: Why Humans Will Live in AI-Created Realities
item.description: AI-generated worlds may satisfy humanity's drive for exploration by creating endless, responsive environments.

item.title: Attractors from the Future: When AI, Consciousness, and Biology Converge
item.description: AI, consciousness, and biology may be converging toward new forms of agency rather than evolving on separate tracks.

item.title: Part 1: When AI Agents Learn to Blackmail: Lessons from Agentic Misalignment Research
item.description: Agentic AI misalignment can emerge when systems pursue instrumental goals such as survival, access, or task completion under pressure.

item.title: Part 2: The Theology of AI Alignment: Why Atheistic Objective Functions Lead to Misalignment
item.description: A draft philosophical argument that survival-maximizing objective functions may encode a hidden moral assumption rather than a neutral technical goal.

item.title: The Secular Religion of the West: Ritual Without God
item.description: Modern secular events can function like religious rituals by transmitting values, identity, belonging, and moral order.

item.title: Is It AI Slop? The Truth About How I Write
item.description: AI-assisted writing can still be authored if the human supplies the taste, argument, selection, and final judgment.

item.title: Riding the Tech Wave: Lessons from Surfing
item.description: AI adoption is framed through surfing: spot the swell, paddle early, ride with balance, and adjust as conditions change.

item.title: Part 1: Your First Time - Getting Comfortable in the Water
item.description: Beginner surfing starts with tolerating cold water, waves, and ocean discomfort before worrying about performance.

item.title: Part 2: Catching White Water - Your First Real Waves
item.description: White-water waves teach timing, board position, and the first feeling of momentum.

item.title: Part 3: The Popup Paradox - Getting to Your Feet
item.description: Standing up is the hard beginner bottleneck after catching waves because the movement has to become automatic under pressure.

item.title: Part 4: Reps Over Reputation - Why Volume Beats Perfection
item.description: Surfing improves through high-rep attempts and quick decisions, not by trying to look polished.

item.title: Youth and Wisdom: A Voice Experiment Across Models
item.description: I gave several models the same youth-versus-wisdom prompt and kept the results. The argument barely changes; the voice does — and that gap is the finding.
